prompts.label: First TypeScript file
prompts.text:
I'm new to TypeScript. Create a minimal setup without a framework.

- hello.ts with a typed greet(name: string) function
- tsconfig.json with strict: true explained in one sentence each for target and module
- Show npx tsc and node hello.js (or ts-node alternative)

Explain the difference from plain JavaScript in 3 bullet points.

prompts.label: Interfaces for a User model
prompts.text:
Teach TypeScript interfaces with a small example.

- interface User { id: number; name: string; email: string; active?: boolean }
- Function formatUser(user: User): string
- One example object and console.log output

Show what happens if I pass a wrong shape (conceptually) and how the compiler helps.

prompts.label: Union types and literals
prompts.text:
Explain union types and string literal types for a beginner.

- type TaskStatus = 'pending' | 'in_progress' | 'done'
- interface Task { id: number; title: string; status: TaskStatus }
- Function setStatus(task: Task, status: TaskStatus): Task

Include one if/switch example that narrows the type.

prompts.label: Generic function firstArray
prompts.text:
Introduce generics with a simple utility.

- function first<T>(items: T[]): T | undefined
- Use it with number[] and string[]
- Add a short comment why we need <T>

No React yet. Keep the file under 30 lines.

prompts.label: Type narrowing with typeof
prompts.text:
Show type narrowing for beginners.

- function printId(id: string | number)
- Use typeof checks before printing
- Mention one common mistake (assuming it's always string)

Provide runnable code and expected output.

prompts.label: const enum or union for roles
prompts.text:
Compare string union types vs const enum for user roles in TypeScript.

- Recommend one approach for a small app and why
- Example: canAccess(role, feature) function
- Keep it beginner-friendly; mention that many teams prefer unions over enum

Provide a single file example.

prompts.label: async/await with typed Promise
prompts.text:
TypeScript + async/await for a beginner.

- interface User { id: number; name: string }
- async function fetchUser(id: number): Promise<User> — simulate delay with setTimeout in a Promise
- async function main() with try/catch

Use Node or a single .ts file runnable with ts-node; say which you chose.

prompts.label: Vite + TypeScript project
prompts.text:
Walk me through creating a Vite project with TypeScript template.

- Exact npm create vite steps
- Explain src/main.ts and index.html relationship
- Change the page to show a typed counter variable
- List scripts: dev, build, preview

Assume I have Node LTS installed.

prompts.label: ESLint + Prettier basics
prompts.text:
For a small TypeScript project, explain how to add ESLint and Prettier (high level, beginner).

- What each tool does in one sentence
- Example .eslintrc or flat config snippet (minimal)
- Example .prettierrc with semi and singleQuote
- Do not over-configure; focus on workflow: save file → format

Mention this is optional for learning but common at work.

prompts.label: unknown vs any vs never
prompts.text:
Explain unknown, any, and never for a TypeScript beginner.

- When to avoid any
- function parseJson(input: string): unknown and a type guard to check shape
- never in exhaustive switch default case example

Keep examples short and practical.

prompts.label: satisfies for config literals
prompts.text:
Explain satisfies X vs as X with a tiny theme/color token object.
Show how satisfies preserves literal types while still type-checking exhaustiveness.
Give a mistaken as assertion example that satisfies catches.
Relate this to const assertion interplay.
Mention VS Code autocomplete benefits for newcomers.

prompts.label: Template literal types for routes
prompts.text:
Teach template literal types that model REST-style route strings.
Start with a union of path segments combined into full paths without manual duplication.
Show how Extract narrows unions of strings.
Mention regex template limitations honestly for beginners.
Keep the example playground-sized (~20 lines) with comments.

prompts.label: Branded types for nominal flavor
prompts.text:
I keep mixing plain strings that represent IDs. Introduce a branding technique with intersection types.
Provide UserId and Email types that cannot be assigned to each other accidentally.
Show a factory function validateUserId(raw: string): UserId.
Explain trade-offs vs schemas like Zod at a glance.
Note that branding is erased at runtime—why that matters.

prompts.label: discriminated union state machines
prompts.text:
Model UI fetch state using discriminated unions with kind: 'idle' | 'loading' | 'success' | 'error'.
Demonstrate narrowing in switch statements.
Contrast with optional fields that overlap confusingly.
Add a helper function transition rules in words.
Relate briefly to Redux style state without going deep.

prompts.label: Index signatures versus Record utility
prompts.text:
Compare { [key: string]: number } vs Record<UserRole, number> vs mapped types.
Explain excess property checking surprises with index signatures.
Give a dictionaries use-case where Record is nicer.
Show keyof limitations when using string index signatures.
Conclude when to reach for Map at runtime.

prompts.label: ReturnType on async functions
prompts.text:
Show ReturnType<<typeof asyncFn>> yielding Promise<number> vs using Awaited<>.
Build a stub API client typing responses from helpers.
Explain why typeof on values matters vs types namespace.
Touch on overloaded function limitations.
Offer a cheatsheet snippet for juniors.

prompts.label: Global augmentation for Window
prompts.text:
I injected a ga('event') snippet and want TS to recognize window.dataLayer safely.
Show a declare global pattern in a shim.d.ts file.
Warn about polluting globals and coordinating with SSR.
Demonstrate narrowing typeof window checks.
Mention DefinitelyTyped motivation without deep dive.

prompts.label: ReadonlyArray and readonly tuples
prompts.text:
Explain why function parameters often want readonly arrays.
Contrast readonly string[] with mutable string[] assignment direction.
Show a tuple like readonly [number, string] for coordinates.
Relate to as const on array literals.
Give an example where mutating a parameter causes a bug tests miss.

prompts.label: unknown vs any practical policy
prompts.text:
Help me write a team rule of thumb: default to unknown at JSON boundaries.
Walk through typeof and Array.isArray narrowing.
Show a simple user-defined type guard function isUser.
Explain why any disables checks and when it's still acceptable temporarily.
Include a before/after on a fetch JSON handler.

prompts.label: tsconfig moduleResolution bundler vs node16
prompts.text:
At a beginner level, explain why new projects pick "moduleResolution": "bundler" under Vite.
Contrast with node16 for libraries publishing to npm.
Link to the package.json "exports" field confusion.
List two symptoms when resolution is wrong (cannot find module).
Give a minimal tsconfig diff checklist.
